meta.name: Chicken Noodle Soup
meta.subtitle: Universal comfort soup • “use what you have” friendly • homemade stock or quick broth path

source.notes: Baseline chicken noodle soup designed for SSA planning: supports leftover chicken, rotisserie, or raw chicken; quick broth or scratch stock; flexible vegetables and noodle options.

yield.notes: Makes about 10–12 cups depending on simmer reduction and noodle choice.

tasteProfile.notes: Clean chicken broth with classic mirepoix and herbs; brightened with lemon and fresh herbs at the end.

ingredients.label: Chicken (see options: bone-in thighs, whole chicken pieces, or cooked/rotisserie)
ingredients.notes: About 2 lb. If using cooked chicken, use ~450g cooked shredded and follow quick broth path.

ingredients.label: Chicken broth or stock (low-sodium preferred)
ingredients.notes: About 8 cups. If doing scratch stock in-pot, water can replace part of this (see steps).

ingredients.label: Water (optional, for scratch stock / dilution)
ingredients.notes: Up to 4 cups as needed if simmering raw chicken to create broth.

ingredients.label: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 teaspoon, plus more to taste.

ingredients.label: Salt (adjust to your broth)
ingredients.notes: Start with ~1 teaspoon if using low-sodium broth; adjust at end.

ingredients.label: Egg noodles (or pasta shapes)
ingredients.notes: About 4 oz dry. Add more for thicker soup; cook separately for best leftovers.

ingredients.label: Leafy greens (optional: spinach/kale/chard), chopped
ingredients.notes: Stir in at the end until wilted.

steps.title: Build the aromatic base
instructions: Heat oil (and butter if using) in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook 6–8 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Choose your chicken path: raw (in-pot stock) or cooked (quick)
instructions: RAW CHICKEN PATH (best flavor): add raw chicken pieces to the pot. Add broth plus enough water to cover chicken if needed. Add bay leaf, thyme, pepper, and a light pinch of salt.

COOKED CHICKEN PATH (fast): skip adding chicken now. Add broth to the pot with bay leaf, thyme, pepper, and a light pinch of salt. Simmer 10 minutes to blend flavors, then add shredded cooked chicken later (Step 4).

steps.title: Simmer until chicken is tender (raw path) or flavors meld (cooked path)
instructions: Bring to a gentle simmer (not a hard boil).
RAW PATH: simmer 25–35 minutes until chicken is cooked and tender (thighs ~30 min). Remove chicken to a bowl.
COOKED PATH: simmer broth 10–15 minutes to build flavor.

steps.title: Shred chicken and return to pot
instructions: If you cooked raw chicken: let it cool briefly, then shred or chop meat. Discard skin and bones (or save bones for stock).
Return shredded chicken to the pot.
Taste broth and adjust salt/pepper (remember noodles absorb salt).

steps.title: Cook noodles (best practice: separate pot for leftovers)
instructions: BEST FOR MEAL PREP/LEFTOVERS: cook noodles separately in salted water until just tender, drain, and add to each bowl when serving.
ONE-POT OPTION: add noodles directly to soup and simmer until tender (usually 6–10 minutes depending on noodle).

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: If using peas, add for the last 3–5 minutes.
If using greens, stir in at the end until wilted (1–2 minutes).
Turn off heat. Add parsley and lemon juice (if using). Taste and adjust seasoning.

variations.name: Use-what-you-have vegetables
variations.change: Swap/add vegetables such as diced potato, zucchini, corn, green beans, mushrooms, or cabbage.
variations.notes: Add firm vegetables earlier; tender vegetables later.

variations.name: Herb-forward version
variations.change: Add fresh dill at the end or replace thyme with Italian seasoning.

variations.name: Creamy chicken noodle (light)
variations.change: Stir in 120g half-and-half or 120g evaporated milk off heat; do not boil after adding dairy.
variations.notes: Add a little more salt to balance.

variations.name: Gluten-free
variations.change: Use gluten-free noodles or rice; cook separately to prevent breakup.
variations.notes: Allergen list changes based on noodle choice.

leftovers.bestPractice: Store noodles separately to avoid mushy leftovers.
leftovers.batching: Freeze base (broth + chicken + veg) without noodles for best texture.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 4 days. Store noodles separately if possible.
storage.freezer: Freeze soup base (without noodles) up to 3 months. Thaw overnight and reheat gently; cook fresh noodles when serving.
storage.reheat: Reheat gently to a simmer; avoid hard boiling to keep chicken tender.

notes: If broth tastes flat: add a squeeze of lemon, a pinch more salt, and/or extra herbs.
If soup is too salty: dilute with water and simmer 5 minutes; adjust aromatics if needed.
If using low-sodium broth, season gradually—noodles and chicken absorb salt.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions; designed for SSA 'use what you have' logic.
